description: Classify insurance companies for outreach. Target P&C carriers and TPAs with high-volume claims operations suitable for AI-powered claims automation.

client.who_we_target: Property & Casualty insurance carriers and TPAs with high-volume claims operations suitable for AI-powered claims automation. Must have 1,000+ employees and significant inbound phone/claims operations.
client.product_focus: AI agents that automate claims processing: FNOL (First Notice of Loss) intake via phone/email, repair estimates for auto/property damage, and faster claims adjudication.

waterfall_filter.description: Cheap pre-qualification filter to quickly disqualify companies that clearly don't fit before running full qualification.

questions.prompt: Is this an insurance carrier, TPA (Third Party Administrator), or claims adjusting firm? Not a broker/agent, software vendor, or consulting firm?
Does this company handle insurance claims (FNOL intake, claims processing, claims adjusting)? Not just underwriting or policy sales?

disqualify_rules.log_message: Not an insurance carrier, TPA, or claims adjuster

waterfall_filter.closing_instruction: Be decisive. If clearly a broker, software vendor, or health insurer, say NO to the relevant question.

product_context.summary: An AI-powered claims automation platform that provides AI voice agents to automate insurance claims calls. The AI handles inbound FNOL calls, processes repair estimates, and speeds up claims resolution.
key_use_cases: Automating FNOL (First Notice of Loss) phone intake - when a customer calls to report an accident or property damage
Processing repair estimates for auto body damage or property repairs
Outbound calls for claims status updates and missing documentation
Reducing call center headcount while improving claims processing speed
product_context.why_p_and_c: P&C insurers (auto, home, property) have HIGH frequency claims with phone-heavy intake processes. Health and life insurers have different workflows that don't fit this product.

categories.name: P_AND_C_CARRIER
categories.description: Property & Casualty insurance carriers that underwrite auto, home, property, or multi-line personal insurance. Must have direct claims operations with high volume.
examples: Allstate - large US auto and home insurer
The Hartford - P&C and auto insurance
AXA - global multi-line with strong P&C
MAPFRE - Spanish P&C insurer with LatAm presence
look_for: Auto insurance products (car insurance, vehicle coverage, collision, comprehensive)
Home/property insurance (homeowners, renters, dwelling, property damage)
Claims filing for accidents, collisions, property damage, theft, weather damage
Repair network partnerships (auto body shops, contractors, glass repair)
Large employee count (1,000+) with dedicated claims staff

categories.name: TPA_CLAIMS_ADJUSTER
categories.description: Third Party Administrators and claims adjusting firms that handle P&C claims on behalf of carriers. Must have high claims volume and 1,000+ employees.
examples: Sedgwick - large TPA handling P&C claims
Gallagher Bassett - TPA for P&C claims
look_for: Auto damage appraisals or repair estimates
FNOL intake services for insurance clients

categories.name: MGA_MGU
categories.description: Managing General Agents/Underwriters that underwrite P&C policies AND handle claims. Must have claims authority, not just underwriting.
examples: AmWINS - large MGA with some claims handling
RT Specialty - MGA with delegated authority
Various regional MGAs with binding authority and claims handling
look_for: Must handle claims, not just underwrite
categories.note: Many MGAs only do underwriting/distribution with no claims role - these are DISQUALIFIED

categories.name: DISQUALIFIED
categories.description: Does not fit the target ICP. See disqualification_rules for specific types.

categories.name: INSUFFICIENT_INFO
categories.description: Cannot determine from available information whether company qualifies.

disqualification_rules.description: Health insurance companies - different claims workflow, not a fit
Life insurance-only companies - low claims frequency, different process
disqualification_rules.note: If a company does BOTH life AND P&C, they may still qualify if P&C operations are significant

disqualification_rules.description: Commercial or specialty insurers only - low volume, complex claims not suited for AI automation
disqualification_rules.note: Companies with BOTH commercial and personal lines may qualify if personal lines are significant

disqualification_rules.description: Reinsurance companies - no direct claims handling with consumers
Insurance brokers or agents that only sell policies - no claims handling
Insurtech or software companies that sell TO insurers - not insurers themselves
Company has fewer than 1,000 employees OR evidence of very low claims volume
Account is on the Do Not Contact list (existing customer or explicitly excluded)

strong_positive: Auto insurance claims - collision, comprehensive, liability claims
Property claims - homeowners, dwelling, fire, water damage, theft
FNOL (First Notice of Loss) processes mentioned
Repair estimates - auto body, property repair, contractor networks
Claims call center or contact center operations
Mobile claims filing apps (indicates high volume)
Auto body shop or contractor networks
positive: Hiring for claims adjusters, claims representatives, call center reps
Multiple locations or large geographic footprint
pages_to_check: Claims page - look for 'File a Claim', 'Report a Claim', 'Claims Process'
Products page - look for auto, home, property insurance products
About page - company size, history, lines of business
Careers page - hiring for claims roles indicates volume
Contact page - claims hotline numbers indicate phone operations

questions.question: Is this a Property & Casualty insurer, TPA, or claims adjuster (handles auto, home, or property insurance/claims)?
Does this company directly handle claims (FNOL intake, claims processing, claims adjusting)?
Does this company specifically handle auto collision claims or property damage claims that involve repair estimates?
Does this company have 1,000+ employees OR clear evidence of high claims volume (large claims team, multiple call centers)?
Is there evidence of phone-based claims operations (claims hotline, FNOL by phone, claims call center)?
Does this company serve personal/consumer lines (individual auto, homeowners) rather than purely commercial?
What type of entity is this: P_AND_C_CARRIER (insurance company), TPA_CLAIMS_ADJUSTER (third party claims handler), or MGA_MGU (managing general agent with claims authority)?

classification_logic.description: Apply these rules IN ORDER to determine final_classification

apply_in_order.if: company is life insurance ONLY (no P&C)

stop_conditions: All confidence values are at least MEDIUM and classification is clear

qualified.description: Large US auto and home insurer with claims hotline
Mexican auto insurer with high claims volume
Global TPA handling P&C claims for multiple carriers
Claims adjusting firm with auto damage appraisals
MGA with delegated claims authority and in-house adjusters

disqualified.description: Life insurance only with no P&C products
Insurance brokerage with no claims operations
Claims software vendor selling to insurers
